failure_scenarios.description: Iran-US ceasefire materializes within 2-4 weeks per ZeroHedge base case, collapsing the geopolitical risk premium in gold while a simultaneous dollar short-squeeze amplifies the drawdown as energy disinflation re-anchors real yield expectations.
failure_scenarios.reasoning: ZeroHedge explicitly frames Tehran negotiations as a near-term base case, not a tail risk. If Hormuz reopens, the two dominant pillars of gold's current bid — energy-driven stagflation fear and safe-haven geopolitical demand — deflate simultaneously. The dollar, which forward_guidance identifies as gold's 'primary transmission mechanism,' would likely stage a short-squeeze as the de-dollarization narrative loses its most acute catalyst, creating a compounding headwind for GLD priced in USD. However, THR_DOLLAR_20260410_001 (ESTABLISHED, conviction 5.8, THREATENING) identifies de-dollarization accelerating via Hormuz disruption.
